clusters.headline: Congo-Kinshasa: DR Congo's Neighbors Boost Preparedness as Ebola Cases Surge
clusters.summary: [DW] Uganda, Rwanda and Zambia are strengthening border screening, disease surveillance and emergency response measures. The growing Ebola outbreak in the DRC is raising concerns about cross-border spread.

clusters.headline: Meet Andy Burnham, Britain's prime minister
clusters.summary: Andy Burnham has been sworn in as the successor to British Prime Minister Keir Starmer. The former Manchester mayor has become a leading figure in the Labour Party and one of the UK's most popular politicians.

clusters.headline: Uganda: New Bundibugyo Ebola Virus Variant Discovered, Offering Critical Insights for Future Outbreak Response
clusters.summary: [Nile Post] Researchers from the Interdisciplinary Consortium for Epidemic Research and Response (ICER) and collaborating institutions have identified a previously unreported variant (novel clade) of the Bundibugyo Ebola virus (BDBV) linked to the ongoing 2026 outbreak in Uganda and the Democratic R

clusters.headline: Africa: A Senegalese Factory Proves Africa Can Spin Its Own Cotton - Can It Find the Money to Keep Going?
clusters.summary: [allAfrica] Domitexka's mill in Kaolack has the markets, the know-how and the partnerships to compete globally. What it still needs -- like nearly every manufacturer on the continent -- is patient capital.

clusters.headline: Africa: Explainer - International Migrants, Jobs and Crime in South Africa: What Do the Numbers Show?
clusters.summary: [Africa Check] EXPLAINER: International migrants, jobs and crime in South Africa: What do the numbers show?

clusters.headline: Some Singapore retailers turn to China for ideas on live commerce and AI as competition intensifies
clusters.summary: After a recent study trip to Hangzhou, some retailers are restructuring teams, investing in technology and redesigning physical stores as they look for new ways to attract shoppers.

clusters.headline: Metro Singapore's history: From Hollywood-inspired name to Orchard Road heyday and final closures
clusters.summary: Metro is set to close its last two remaining department stores in Paragon and Causeway Point malls as part of a retail overhaul, marking a significant shift in the retail giant's history.

clusters.headline: Nigeria: Breaking - Captured 'Ansaru Leaders' Make U-Turn, Plead Guilty to 32 Terrorism Charges
clusters.summary: [Premium Times] National Security Adviser Nuhu Ribadu announced the arrest of the defendants in August 2025.

clusters.headline: Gabon: France Welcomes Gabon's Oligui As Mineral and Military Ties Defy Wider Rift in Africa
clusters.summary: [RFI] Gabon's President Brice Clotaire Oligui Nguema begins a three-day official visit to France on Monday, underlining the close relationship between Paris and one of its few remaining key allies in Francophone Africa.

clusters.headline: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers
clusters.summary: Iran says it has hit two ships in the Strait of Hormuz as well as targets in Bahrain and Jordan.

clusters.headline: Madagascar: Madagascar's Wave of Disappearances Exposes 'Deeper Institutional Failings'
clusters.summary: [RFI] A recent surge in disappearances and violent killings on the Indian Ocean island of Madagascar has fuelled fears across the country and prompted the government to deploy hundreds of security personnel. Anti-corruption campaigner Ketakandriana Rafitoson, of Transparency International Initiative

clusters.headline: India's Gen Z 'Cockroach People's Party' started as satire but is serious about change
clusters.summary: They call themselves the "cockroaches" and they're on the march — demanding action against corruption, amid a shortage of opportunities for young people in India.
